<a id="class-visualshadernodeinput"></a>

# VisualShaderNodeInput

**Inherits:** [VisualShaderNode](class_visualshadernode.md#class-visualshadernode) **<** [Resource](class_resource.md#class-resource) **<** [RefCounted](class_refcounted.md#class-refcounted) **<** [Object](class_object.md#class-object)

Represents the input shader parameter within the visual shader graph.

## Description

Gives access to input variables (built-ins) available for the shader. See the shading reference for the list of available built-ins for each shader type (check `Tutorials` section for link).

## Tutorials

- [Shading reference index](../tutorials/shaders/shader_reference/index.md)

## Signals

<a id="class-visualshadernodeinput-signal-input-type-changed"></a>

**input_type_changed**() [🔗](#class-visualshadernodeinput-signal-input-type-changed)

## Property Descriptions

<a id="class-visualshadernodeinput-property-input-name"></a>

[String](class_string.md#class-string) **input_name** = `"[None]"` [🔗](#class-visualshadernodeinput-property-input-name)

-  **set_input_name**(value: [String](class_string.md#class-string))
- [String](class_string.md#class-string) **get_input_name**()

One of the several input constants in lower-case style like: "vertex" (`VERTEX`) or "point_size" (`POINT_SIZE`).

---

## Method Descriptions

<a id="class-visualshadernodeinput-method-get-input-real-name"></a>

[String](class_string.md#class-string) **get_input_real_name**()  [🔗](#class-visualshadernodeinput-method-get-input-real-name)

Returns a translated name of the current constant in the Godot Shader Language. E.g. `"ALBEDO"` if the [input_name](#class-visualshadernodeinput-property-input-name) equal to `"albedo"`.
